extraction.one_line_summary: SOUPE Joffrey launched from 250m out with no organized leadout train, capitalizing on disorganized sprint to take first Grand Tour win at age 35.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD sprinted intermediate bonus sprint to claim 2 seconds, closing gap to EVENEPOEL to 3 seconds, signaling aggressive GC tactics.
SOUPE launched 250m sprint when no single team controlled leadout, exploiting disorganized finale that typically favors organized trains.
GROVES Kaden lost leadout positioning into final corner despite having teammates earlier, suggesting tactical breakdown under pressure.
Total Energies placed sprint from distance when traditional leadout trains fractured, rare successful tactic for long-range efforts.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: actively contested intermediate sprint for 2-second bonus despite being non-sprinter, showing intent to pressure EVENEPOEL on time.
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: finished fifth with leadout advantage early but lost positioning in final corner, tactical execution failure.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: stopped for mechanical with teammates, described as worst GC rider entering race, mental fragility evident after knee crash.
rider_signals.SOUPE Geoffrey: positive: veteran leadout man converted disorganized finale into career-best win, opportunistic positioning when favorites lacked organization.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Lenny: positive: Groupama-FDJ successfully protected first-ever Grand Tour leader jersey throughout opening roads, team executed defensive protocol.
